slides.title: The quiet plant compound that *protects your bones*.
slides.sub: Soy isoflavones act like gentle phytoestrogens. They may help women age with stronger bones and steadier menopausal changes.

slides.title: Meet the isoflavones
slides.body: Compounds in soy, like *genistein*, loosely mimic estrogen. They are called phytoestrogens because they fit the same receptors, gently.

slides.title: Estrogen guards your bones
slides.body: As estrogen falls in menopause, bone loss can speed up. Isoflavones *may help preserve* bone density during that shift.

slides.title: Easier menopausal changes
slides.body: Studies suggest soy isoflavones *can support* fewer hot flashes and a smoother transition for some women through menopause.

slides.title: Food-level soy looks safe
slides.body: Large reviews find dietary soy *is not linked to* higher breast-cancer risk. For many women it appears protective.

slides.title: Choose whole soy foods
slides.body: Tofu, edamame, tempeh and soy milk are *simple daily sources*. A serving or two a day fits most balanced plates.

slides.title: Turn small habits into *strong years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs the food, movement, steps and sleep that protect your bones and rolls them into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Bone strength in menopause often comes down to the small things you eat every day. Soy is one of the gentlest. 🌿

Isoflavones like genistein act as mild phytoestrogens that may help preserve bone density and ease the menopausal transition, and food-level soy is not linked to higher breast-cancer risk. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily food and movement into one simple score → link in bio.
